title: Advanced Phrasal Verbs — C1
topics: formal contexts · abstract meanings · work · communication
description: Pokročilá frázová slovesa na úrovni C1 — formální, pracovní a abstraktní kontexty.
notes: ✓ account FOR: 'How do you account for the discrepancy?'
✓ boil DOWN TO: 'It all boils down to trust.' (= the essential point is)
✓ draw ON: 'She drew on years of expertise.'
✓ come to TERMS WITH: always plural 'terms'
✓ fall short OF (not 'from'): 'Sales fell short of targets.'
✓ live up TO: 'It didn't live up to our expectations.'
⚠️  C1 phrasal verbs often have formal single-word equivalents:
account for = explain | stem from = originate | elaborate on = expand

examples.en: How do you account for the sudden drop in sales?
The problem stems from a lack of communication.
She drew on her experience as a nurse to write the novel.
examples.cz: Čerpala ze svých zkušeností při psaní románu.

examples.en: They're phasing out diesel engines by 2030.
examples.cz: Do roku 2030 postupně vyřadí dieselové motory.

examples.en: Could you elaborate on that last point?
The results fell short of what we had hoped for.
It all boils down to one question: can we afford it?
examples.cz: Vše se redukuje na jednu otázku: můžeme si to dovolit?

examples.en: She pulled off a remarkable comeback in the final set.
examples.cz: Ve finálním setu předvedla pozoruhodný obrat.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: The report doesn't account for the Q3 losses — have you looked into it?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: Yes. The losses stem from supply chain disruptions. I'll elaborate on that in the meeting.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: Good. Don't gloss over the bad news — the board needs the full picture.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: Understood. I'll back up the figures with the raw data.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: And make sure you follow through on the action points from last quarter.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: Will do. Full analysis ready by Thursday.

quiz.sentence: Could you ___ on your earlier point about costs?
Sales ___ short of our targets for Q2.
She drew ___ years of experience when writing the proposal.
They plan to ___ out fossil fuels by 2035.

tasks: 1. Write 10 sentences using phrasal verbs from the vocabulary section — use real-life contexts
2. Replace with phrasal verbs: 'She minimised the problem.' / 'It originated from budget cuts.'
3. Write a professional email using at least 5 phrasal verbs from this lesson
